title: Enforce verify lifecycle ordering for commit_if_verified
summary: Ensure verify.start precedes verify results and gate commit_if_verified on a successful verify lifecycle with clear errors.
acceptance_criteria: `commit_if_verified` rejects calls made before `verify.start` with a clear error.
A valid verify lifecycle (`verify.start` → verify result) allows `commit_if_verified` to proceed.
Ordering checks are enforced consistently across verify/commit tool calls.
Error messages explicitly mention missing `verify.start` when applicable.

steps.description: Locate all verify lifecycle emitters and consumers in the tool flow.
Add ordering guards so verify results are only accepted after `verify.start`.
Gate `commit_if_verified` on a successful verify lifecycle and return clear errors otherwise.
Add/adjust tests to cover correct ordering and error paths.

notes: Keep changes minimal and localized to verify/commit ordering enforcement.
Avoid unrelated refactors while tightening sequencing checks.
